page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by AWS Graviton ARM64 processors. The system features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance is designed without local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network and Elastic Block Store (EBS) operations. The high-density ARM64 architecture offers resource efficiency for workloads requiring massive parallel processing capabilities without the overhead of hyperthreading.

Suitable workloads for the m8gn.metal-48xl include large-scale general-purpose applications, high-throughput network appliances, distributed data stores, and containerized microservices that benefit from high core counts and substantial network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton ARM64 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory to deliver a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but is highly optimized for network and EBS performance, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. This high-density, non-hyperthreaded architecture provides efficient resource allocation for parallel processing tasks. It is well-suited for high-throughput network applications, distributed databases, containerized microservices, and large-scale general-purpose workloads requiring significant network bandwidth and high core counts.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated ARM64 cores, 768 GB of RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth optimized for general-purpose and network-heavy workloads.
